init.template: Ask the user which locale they want for this project (pt for Portuguese, es for Spanish, or en for English). Then initialize the `.opencode/` project configuration in the current working directory by running the init script with the chosen locale. The script will detect project languages, suggest LSPs, and optionally configure `.vscode/settings.json`. After init completes, verify `.opencode/locale` contains the chosen language. Then review the generated files.
init.description: Initialize opencode project config with repo context and locale

ocf:plan-feature.template: Break down the feature into steps, identify impacted layers, and register risks and planned work in `.opencode/known_issues.md`.

ocf:promote.template: Promote a tracked issue in `known_issues.md`. Read data from the issue entry — if something essential is missing, handle gracefully without asking: `Base branch:` empty → detect from git; `Reviewers:` empty → default to 1; `Remote:` empty → create automatically. If status is `backlog`: run `scripts/promote.sh <id>` (→ ready, status only). If status is `ready`: ensure `Remote:` is populated (auto-create if missing), then run `scripts/promote.sh <id>` (→ in-progress + branch). Other statuses: refuse with reason.
ocf:promote.description: Promote an issue — backlog→ready or ready→in-progress with branch

ocf:review-branch.template: Read `- Reviewers:` from the issue entry in `known_issues.md` to determine how many Senior Reviewers should perform this review. If the field is missing or empty, default to 1 — do NOT ask the user unless the field is absent. Do NOT read reviewer count from opencode.json. Then run a full PR/MR-style review: analyze `git diff`, use that many Senior Engineer reviewers, identify Critical and Major issues only, validate tests and docs, and register new issues.

ocf:develop.template:
Detect the issue ID from the argument, or from the current branch name (`issue-<id>-<slug>`), or ask only if neither is available. Read the issue entry from `known_issues.md`. Check for missing data and handle gracefully:
- `Base branch:` empty → detect from git (origin/HEAD → main/master fallback), no prompt
- `Reviewers:` empty → default to 1, no prompt
- `Business rules:` empty for `feat` type → warn user it will be blocked by Committer gate later
- `Remote:` empty and status needs it → auto-create via `scripts/create_issue.sh <id>`, no prompt

Then auto-promote based on status:

- `backlog`: `scripts/promote.sh <id>` (→ ready) → `scripts/create_issue.sh <id>` (remote) → `scripts/promote.sh <id>` (→ in-progress + branch)
- `ready`: if Remote is `-`, create it first. Then `scripts/promote.sh <id>` (→ in-progress + branch)
- `open` with Remote set: update status to `in-progress`, checkout/create branch `issue-<id>-<slug>`
- `open` with `Remote: -`: `scripts/create_issue.sh <id>` (→ in-progress + branch)
- `in-progress`: proceed directly
- `in-review` / `in-qa` / `in-publish` / `resolved`: refuse — issue is past development

After promotion, verify the current git branch matches `issue-<id>-<slug>`; if not, checkout or create it. Invoke the Developer agent with full issue context (title, description, business rules, acceptance criteria, location, branch). After the Developer finishes implementation, update status to \`in-review\` and automatically proceed to Senior Review — do NOT pause, do NOT ask the user for confirmation, do NOT prompt about creating an MR/PR. The pipeline is continuous.
ocf:develop.description: Start or resume development on an issue, auto-promoting if needed

ocf:scan-issues.template: Run a deep analysis of the codebase to detect new issues (security, concurrency, architecture, reliability). Create or update `.opencode/known_issues.md`. Follow the issue entry format from `standards/issues.md`.
ocf:scan-issues.description: Deep codebase analysis and issue detection

ocf:commit.template: Create an atomic semantic commit following `standards/commits.md`. Use format `<type>(<scope>): <imperative description>` with `Issue: #<id>` and `Status:` trailers. Ask for type/scope/description/issue-id. Detect the project known_issues.md, construct the commit, run `git commit`, and update known_issues.md status based on trailers. Split if the change covers multiple concerns.

ocf:sync-issues.template: Scan known_issues.md entries with Remote: references, fetch remote issue state via gh/glab, sync status (close remote if local resolved, reopen if mismatch), create remote issues for open entries without remote, and report sync results.
ocf:sync-issues.description: Sync known_issues with remote issue tracker

ocf:archive-issue.template: Move a resolved issue from known_issues.md to resolved_issues.md in compact format. The issue must be Status: resolved. Use close_issue.sh which handles both closing and archiving in one step.
ocf:archive-issue.description: Archive resolved issue to compact format

ocf:maintain.template: Run full maintenance on known_issues.md and prioritization. Resolve `prioritization.md` relative to the project root first (`.opencode/prioritization.md`), falling back to `~/.config/opencode/prioritization.md` if the project has no local copy. First run `$SCRIPTS_DIR/maintain.sh` to detect stale issues (local in-progress but remote closed) and status mismatches. Then for each stale issue, run `close_issue.sh <id>` to archive. Reindex remaining issues. Report what was archived/updated.

ocf:close-issue.template: Detect the issue ID from the argument, or from the current branch name (`issue-<id>-<slug>`), or ask if neither is available. Read the issue entry from `known_issues.md`. The issue must have `Remote: #<id>` and `Status: in-publish`. Verify the PR is merged: if `- PR: #<n>` field exists, run `gh pr view <n> --json state --jq '.state'`; if not merged, report state and abort. If no PR field, search merged PRs by branch name matching `issue-<remote-id>-*`. If PR is merged, run `scripts/close_issue.sh <id>` — this closes the remote issue on GitHub/GitLab and archives the entry to `resolved_issues.md` in compact format. Report the result.
ocf:close-issue.description: Close remote issue and archive after PR merge

ocf:check-pr.template:
Check PR merge status for issues. Ask the user for an issue ID, or scan all issues with `Status: in-publish`. For each issue:
1. If the issue has a `- PR:` field, use `gh pr view <pr-number> --json state,mergedAt` directly.
2. If no PR field, search for merged PRs referencing the issue:
   - First try: `gh pr list --search "Issue: #<local-id> in:body" --state merged --json number,headRefName,mergedAt`
   - If that fails (or is ambiguous), list all merged PRs with `gh pr list --state merged --json number,headRefName,mergedAt --limit 30` and filter by branch name matching the Remote ID pattern `issue-<remote-id>-`.
3. If a unique merged PR is found:
   - Add a `- PR: #<number>` field to the issue entry
   - Update status to `resolved`
   - Run `close_issue.sh <id>` to archive to `resolved_issues.md`
   - Report PR number and merge date
4. If no merged PR found, report the PR state (open/closed/not found).
5. For issues without `Remote:` field, skip with a warning.
ocf:check-pr.description: Check PR merge status for issues and auto-archive merged ones

ocf:review-external.template: Ask the user for the MR URL (GitHub/GitLab) or remote branch name to review. If nothing is provided, show usage: `ocf:review-external <mr-url|remote-branch>`. Parse the input: if an MR URL is provided, extract the repo and branch info; if a remote branch is provided, use it directly. Fetch the remote branch and check it out locally for analysis. Then run the review-external subagent to perform a structured review across all senior reviewer domains (backend, frontend, security, data, devops, performance, runtime, mobile, ux-ui, qa), classify findings (critical/high/medium/low/nit), generate a report saved to `.opencode/reviews/review-<branch>-<timestamp>.md`, and optionally post critical/high findings to the MR. Present the report path and a summary of findings to the user.
ocf:review-external.description: Review external branches/MRs with structured report generation

ocf:bump-version.template:
Calculate version bump, update changelog, commit, tag, and publish to main. Steps:

1. Read VERSION file → get current version. Find the latest git tag matching `v*` (e.g. v1.2.0). Compare VERSION with the tag version to determine baseline.

2. Analyze commits since the last tag via `git log <last-tag>..HEAD --oneline`. Classify each commit:
   - `feat!:` or `BREAKING CHANGE:` → breaking (major)
   - `feat:` → feature (minor)
   - `fix:`, `refactor:`, `chore:`, `docs:`, `test:` → patch-level
   - No meaningful changes → no bump needed, abort

3. Calculate bump type from the highest classification found. Show user:
   - Current VERSION: X.Y.Z
   - Last tag: vX.Y.Z
   - Commits analyzed: N (list types found)
   - Suggested new version: X.Y.Z + 1

4. Ask user to confirm the new version. They can accept, override with a different version, or cancel.

5. If confirmed:
   a. Update VERSION file with new version
   b. Update README.md version badge (line 3: `**Version:** X.Y.Z`)
   c. Ask user for a one-line changelog entry title describing the changes
   d. Read CHANGELOG.md, prepend a new section after the header:
      `## X.Y.Z (YYYY-MM-DD)\n\n- <user's title>`
   e. `git add VERSION README.md CHANGELOG.md`
   f. Commit: `chore: bump version to X.Y.Z`
   g. Create annotated tag: `git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m "Version X.Y.Z"`
   h. Push commit + tags: `git push origin <current-branch> --tags`
   i. Create GitHub Release: extract the CHANGELOG section for this version and run `gh release create vX.Y.Z --title "vX.Y.Z" --notes "<changelog section>"`
   j. If current branch is NOT main: create a PR to main via `gh pr create --base main --head <current-branch> --title "chore: bump version to X.Y.Z" --body "Automated version bump."`
      If current branch IS main: push directly (already done in step h)

6. Report the result: new version, commit hash, tag, Release URL, and PR URL (if created).
ocf:bump-version.description: Calculate and apply version bump, update changelog, commit, tag, and publish to main

ocf:backup.template: Run `$SCRIPTS_DIR/backup.sh` with the provided arguments. Ask the user for: 1) source directory, 2) backup name (default dev_backup), 3) whether to create a .zip. Then execute the backup. After completion, report the backup path, size, and exclusions applied.
ocf:backup.description: Create intelligent timestamped backup excluding junk
